 {"id":"caam-4eg","title":"Browser-Profile Aware Login","description":"## Overview
The PRIMARY pain point for users with multiple 'all you can eat' subscriptions is the OAuth login flow. When you have 5 Claude Max accounts (each with a different Google account), the browser OAuth flow is painful because:
1. Browser opens with your 'default' Google account
2. You have to click 'switch account'
3. Find the right account from the list
4. Then authorize

This takes 30-60 seconds and is error-prone (logging into wrong account).

## Solution
Associate each caam profile with a specific browser profile (e.g., Chrome profile). When login flows need a browser, open the URL in THAT browser profile, which already has the correct Google account logged in.

## Key Insight
Chrome (and Firefox) support launching with a specific profile directory. This is the same mechanism that allows people to have separate 'work' and 'personal' browser profiles. We leverage this to make OAuth flows automatically use the right Google account.

## Implementation Approach
1. Add browser profile config to Profile struct (browser_command, browser_profile_dir)
2. Create browser launcher abstraction
3. Detect localhost redirect URLs from CLI stdout
4. Launch browser with correct profile when URL detected
5. Support Chrome, Firefox, and 'default browser' fallback

## User Value
Reduces login friction from 30-60 seconds to ~5 seconds (just click authorize). Eliminates risk of logging into wrong account.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:49:43.32942877-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:22:09.970282351-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:22:09.970282351-05:00","close_reason":"Browser-Profile Aware Login epic complete. All 5 child tasks done: Profile struct browser config, browser launcher abstraction, URL detection, provider integration, and CLI flags."}

 {"id":"caam-4ve","title":"TUI hot reload integration","description":"## Purpose
Integrate the file system watcher with the TUI to automatically refresh the profile
list when files change on disk.

## Background
This enables a seamless workflow where:
1. User has TUI open in Terminal 1
2. User runs `caam backup claude newprofile` in Terminal 2
3. TUI automatically shows the new profile without restart

codex-pool achieves similar with `POST /admin/reload`. For caam, we use file watching.

## User Experience
```
# Terminal 1: TUI running
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────────────────┐
│ ● work@company.com    🟢 Active       │
│   personal@gmail.com  🟡              │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘

# Terminal 2: Add new profile
$ caam backup claude newproject@corp.com
Profile saved.

# Terminal 1: TUI auto-updates (no restart needed!)
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────────────────┐
│ ● work@company.com       🟢 Active    │
│   personal@gmail.com     🟡           │
│   newproject@corp.com    🟢 NEW       │  ← appeared automatically
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Technical Implementation

### Message Type
```go
// internal/tui/messages.go
type profilesChangedMsg struct {
    event watcher.Event
}
```

### Model Integration
```go
// internal/tui/model.go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields
    watcher *watcher.Watcher
}

// Init starts the file watcher
func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
    return tea.Batch(
        m.loadProfiles(),
        m.watchProfiles(),
    )
}

// watchProfiles returns a command that listens for file changes
func (m Model) watchProfiles() tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        select {
        case event := <-m.watcher.Events():
            return profilesChangedMsg{event: event}
        case err := <-m.watcher.Errors():
            return errMsg{err: err}
        }
    }
}

// Update handles the change event
func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case profilesChangedMsg:
        // Show brief notification
        m.statusMsg = fmt.Sprintf("Profile %s %s", 
            msg.event.Profile, 
            eventTypeVerb(msg.event.Type))
        
        // Reload profiles and continue watching
        return m, tea.Batch(
            m.loadProfiles(),
            m.watchProfiles(),
        )
    }
}
```

### Visual Feedback
```go
// Show "NEW" badge briefly for added profiles
type profileBadge struct {
    profile  string
    badge    string
    expiry   time.Time
}

func (m *Model) addBadge(profile, badge string, duration time.Duration) {
    m.badges[profile] = profileBadge{
        profile: profile,
        badge:   badge,
        expiry:  time.Now().Add(duration),
    }
}
```

### Graceful Degradation
```go
// If watcher fails, TUI still works but without auto-refresh
func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
    cmds := []tea.Cmd{m.loadProfiles()}
    
    w, err := watcher.New(m.profilesDir)
    if err != nil {
        // Log warning but continue without watching
        log.Printf("Warning: file watching unavailable: %v", err)
    } else {
        m.watcher = w
        cmds = append(cmds, m.watchProfiles())
    }
    
    return tea.Batch(cmds...)
}
```

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/model.go
- internal/tui/messages.go
- internal/tui/profiles_panel.go (for badges)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New profiles appear automatically in TUI
- [ ] Modified profiles trigger refresh
- [ ] Deleted profiles are removed from list
- [ ] Brief notification shows what changed
- [ ] NEW badge shown for 5 seconds on additions
- [ ] No crash if watcher is unavailable
- [ ] Clean shutdown stops watcher","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:24:29.724093683-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T19:41:51.401036395-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:41:51.401036395-05:00","close_reason":"Wired profile watcher into TUI for hot reload; load vault profiles, show NEW badge, and cleanly close watcher.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-4ve","depends_on_id":"caam-0gx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:25.420647662-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"caam-4ve","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting TUI hot reload integration: wire internal/watcher into Bubble Tea model to reload vault profile list on fs events, show brief status + NEW badge on additions, and ensure watcher closes cleanly on quit. (Agent mail not configured in this env; using issue comments.)","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:32:52Z"},{"id":14,"issue_id":"caam-4ve","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented hot reload in TUI: added fsnotify watcher on vault dir, Bubble Tea cmds/messages to reload profiles on add/modify/delete, vault-backed loadProfiles (with active profile detection), and 5s NEW badge on additions w/ expiry. Added unit tests for vault loading + badge lifecycle. Quit closes watcher; Run also closes as safety.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:41:41Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-m5a","title":"Display QR code for device auth URL (nice-to-have)","description":"# Display QR Code for Device Auth URL

## What

When using device code flow, display a QR code that users can scan with their phone to quickly reach the authorization URL.

## Why

QR codes provide faster mobile access:
- No typing long URLs on phones
- Works even when URL can't be copy-pasted (some SSH clients)
- Professional/polished UX
- Many CLI tools (gh, az) do this successfully

## Implementation

### Dependencies

Use a pure-Go QR code library to avoid external dependencies:
- github.com/mdp/qrterminal (MIT license, popular)
- github.com/skip2/go-qrcode (generates image, not terminal)

### File: internal/auth/qrcode.go

\`\`\`go
package auth

import (
    "io"
    "os"
    
    "github.com/mdp/qrterminal/v3"
    "golang.org/x/term"
)

// DisplayQRCode prints a QR code to the terminal if stdout is a TTY
func DisplayQRCode(url string, w io.Writer) {
    // Only show QR if we have a TTY (not piped/redirected)
    if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())) {
        return
    }
    
    // Use ASCII mode for compatibility
    config := qrterminal.Config{
        Level:     qrterminal.L,
        Writer:    w,
        BlackChar: qrterminal.WHITE, // Inverted for dark terminals
        WhiteChar: qrterminal.BLACK,
        QuietZone: 1,
    }
    qrterminal.GenerateWithConfig(url, config)
}
\`\`\`

### Integration in device code flow

\`\`\`go
func (p *Provider) loginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, prof *profile.Profile) error {
    fmt.Println("Starting device code authentication...")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // For Codex, we need to capture the output to get the code
    // Then display our enhanced version
    
    // Display QR code pointing to the auth URL
    authURL := "https://auth.openai.com/codex/device"
    auth.DisplayQRCode(authURL, os.Stdout)
    
    fmt.Println("")
    fmt.Printf("Open: %s\\n", authURL)
    fmt.Println("Or scan the QR code above with your phone")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // Run the actual codex login --device-auth
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "codex", "login", "--device-auth")
    // ...
}
\`\`\`

## Output Example

\`\`\`
Starting device code authentication...

  █▀▀▀▀▀█ ▀▀▀▀█▀ █▀▀▀▀▀█
  █ ███ █ █▀▄█ ▀ █ ███ █
  █ ▀▀▀ █ ▀▄▀ ▄█ █ ▀▀▀ █
  ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀ ▀ █ ▀ ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀
  ██▀▀▀▀▀ ▀█▄ ▄▀▄▀█▄▀▄██
  ...

Open: https://auth.openai.com/codex/device
Or scan the QR code above with your phone

Enter code: ABCD-EFGH
\`\`\`

## UX Considerations

1. **Terminal compatibility**: QR codes may not render well in all terminals
   - Detect terminal capabilities if possible
   - Provide --no-qr flag to disable

2. **Color inversion**: Dark vs light terminals need different rendering
   - Try to auto-detect (COLORFGBG env var)
   - Provide --qr-invert flag

3. **Size**: QR code shouldn't overwhelm the terminal
   - Keep it reasonably sized
   - Use minimal quiet zone

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] QR code displays for device auth URL
- [ ] Works in common terminals (iTerm2, Terminal.app, GNOME Terminal)
- [ ] Gracefully skipped if not a TTY
- [ ] --no-qr flag to disable
- [ ] URL still printed as text (accessibility)

## Testing

- Visual test in various terminals
- Test with screen reader (ensure URL is still readable)
- Test when stdout is piped (QR should not appear)

## Notes

This is a polish/UX enhancement. Core device code flow should work without this.
Consider deferring until after core device code is working.

## Dependencies

- caam-ela: Codex device code implementation
- caam-v89: CLI --device-code flag","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:39:18.382441283-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:45.861881769-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:45.861881769-05:00","close_reason":"Removed as gold-plating. Codex already displays the URL clearly. QR code adds dependency and complexity for marginal UX gain. Keep caam simple.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-ela","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:40:18.087310678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-v89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:40:23.188174111-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"caam-m9g","title":"EPIC: Just Works UX - Zero-friction account switching","description":"## Vision
Transform CAAM from "useful CLI tool" to "invisible magic that just works."

## Success Criteria
- New user productive in <2 minutes (vs current 10+ minutes)
- Daily use requires zero caam commands (shell wrapper handles everything)
- Rate limit recovery is automatic (detect, cooldown, switch, retry)
- Profile management is intuitive (aliases, workspaces, fuzzy matching)

## Core Features (Priority Order)

### P0 - Foundation (Must complete first)
- [x] **caam-q9f**: Wrapper Mode - `caam run <tool>` with auto rate-limit detection and retry
- [x] **caam-bxq**: Init Wizard - Guided setup with auth discovery

### P1 - Shell Integration (Enables invisible UX)
- [ ] **caam-k9z**: Shell integration - aliases, completion, prompt, auto-activate
- [ ] **caam-hxk**: `caam next` - One-command profile rotation

### P2 - Power Features
- [ ] **caam-08w**: Profile aliases and fuzzy matching
- [ ] **caam-t5p**: Profile workspaces - Switch all tools at once
- [ ] **caam-lr2**: `caam add` - One-command account capture
- [ ] **caam-ah4**: Auth file watcher with auto-backup

### P3 - Polish
- [ ] **caam-vog**: `caam verify` - Validate all profile tokens
- [ ] **caam-0l0**: Background daemon for auto-refresh and sync

## Dependency Graph
```
                    ┌─────────────────┐
                    │   caam-m9g      │
                    │  (this epic)    │
                    └────────┬────────┘
                             │
        ┌────────────────────┼────────────────────┐
        │                    │                    │
        ▼                    ▼                    ▼
   ┌─────────┐         ┌─────────┐         ┌─────────┐
   │caam-q9f │         │caam-bxq │         │caam-hxk │
   │ wrapper │         │  init   │         │  next   │
   └────┬────┘         └─────────┘         └─────────┘
        │
        ▼
   ┌─────────┐
   │caam-k9z │ (shell integration uses wrapper)
   │  shell  │
   └─────────┘
```

## Design Principles
1. **Invisible by default** - Works without thinking about it
2. **Progressive disclosure** - Simple commands, advanced options available
3. **Safe by default** - Auto-backup, no data loss
4. **Fast feedback** - Clear status, quick operations

## Anti-patterns to Avoid
- Requiring users to understand internal architecture
- Multi-step workflows for common operations
- Silent failures or unclear error messages
- Configuration complexity

## What "Just Works" Means

**Before (current state):**
```bash
# User hits rate limit in Claude
# Manually notices the error
caam cooldown set claude/work
caam ls claude  # remember what profiles exist
caam activate claude personal
claude "retry my prompt"
```

**After (with this epic complete):**
```bash
claude "do something"
# Rate limit happens automatically handled
# User sees: "⚠️ Rate limit. Switching to 'personal'..."
# Command continues with new profile
```

## Implementation Order
1. **caam-q9f** (Wrapper Mode) - Core capability
2. **caam-bxq** (Init Wizard) - Onboarding experience
3. **caam-k9z** (Shell Integration) - Makes wrapper invisible
4. **caam-hxk** (caam next) - Quick manual switching
5. P2/P3 features in any order

## Measuring Success
- Time to first profile saved: target <2 minutes
- Daily caam commands required: target 0 (shell wrapper handles all)
- Rate limit recovery time: target <5 seconds (automatic)
- User confusion incidents: target 0 (intuitive UX)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:02:55.725871327-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T21:50:19.217733098-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T21:50:19.217733098-05:00","close_reason":"All sub-items complete: P0 (wrapper, init), P1 (shell, next), P2 (aliases, workspaces, add, authwatch), P3 (verify, daemon). Zero-friction account switching achieved."}

 {"id":"caam-m9ux","title":"Unit tests for sync package (multi-machine sync)","description":"# Sync Package Unit Tests

## Current State

## Test Requirements

### 1. Pool Management Tests

- Load() from file
- Save() to file (atomic)
- AddMachine() validation
- RemoveMachine() cleanup

### 2. Machine Configuration Tests

- SSH connection string parsing
- rsync options handling

### 3. Sync Operation Tests
- Sync() to single machine
- SyncAll() to all machines
- Error handling per machine
- Progress reporting

### 4. State Persistence Tests
- State file format
- Concurrent access safety
- Corruption recovery

### 5. rsync Integration Tests
- Command construction
- Include/exclude patterns
- Dry-run mode
- Error output parsing

## Implementation Notes
- Use local rsync for testing (no remote SSH)
- Test with temp directories as "machines"
- Verify file transfer integrity

 {"id":"caam-mef9","title":"Unit tests for internal/logs package with verbose output","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for internal/logs Package

## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for all log scanners (Claude, Codex, Gemini) with verbose output for debugging.

## Test Categories

### 1. Scanner Interface Tests
```go
func TestScannerInterface(t *testing.T) {
    scanners := []logs.Scanner{
        logs.NewClaudeScanner(),
        logs.NewCodexScanner(),
        logs.NewGeminiScanner(),
    }
    for _, s := range scanners {
        t.Run(s.Provider(), func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] Testing scanner: %s", s.Provider())
            t.Logf("[TEST] Default log dir: %s", s.LogDir())
            // ... interface compliance tests ...
        })
    }
}
```

### 2. JSONL Parsing Tests
For each provider:
- Valid single entry
- Valid multiple entries
- Entry with missing fields (graceful degradation)
- Malformed JSON line (should log error, continue)
- Empty file
- File with only invalid lines
- Unicode in model names/content

### 3. Token Extraction Tests
```go
func TestTokenExtraction(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct{
        name     string
        input    string
        wantInput  int64
        wantOutput int64
        wantCacheRead int64
        wantCacheCreate int64
    }{
        {
            name: "all token types present",
            input: `{"usage":{"input_tokens":100,"output_tokens":200,...}}`,
            wantInput: 100,
            wantOutput: 200,
            ...
        },
        // ... more cases
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] Input: %s", tc.input)
            result := parseEntry(tc.input)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Parsed: input=%d output=%d", result.InputTokens, result.OutputTokens)
            // assertions
        })
    }
}
```

### 4. Time Filtering Tests
- Filter by "since" timestamp
- Edge case: entry exactly at since time
- Edge case: all entries before since time
- Edge case: all entries after since time

### 5. Directory Handling Tests
- Empty directory
- Non-existent directory
- Permission denied
- Symlinks
- Nested directories

## Logging Requirements
Each test MUST:
- Log test name and category at start
- Log all input data
- Log parsed output
- Log any errors with context
- Log assertion results

Example output:
```
=== RUN   TestClaudeScanner/valid_entry
    [TEST] Category: JSONL Parsing
    [TEST] Input file: testdata/valid_claude.jsonl
    [TEST] Line 1: {"timestamp":"2025-01-10T12:00:00Z",...}
    [TEST] Parsed: model=claude-3-opus tokens=1234
    [TEST] Assertion: input_tokens == 1234 PASS
=== PASS
```

## Test Fixtures
Create testdata/ directory with:
- valid_claude.jsonl
- valid_codex.jsonl
- valid_gemini.jsonl
- malformed_lines.jsonl
- empty.jsonl
- mixed_valid_invalid.jsonl

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% code coverage on parsing logic
- [ ] All edge cases tested
- [ ] Verbose logging for debugging
- [ ] Testdata fixtures committed
- [ ] Tests run in <2 seconds

## Files to Create
- internal/logs/claude_test.go
- internal/logs/codex_test.go
- internal/logs/gemini_test.go
- internal/logs/scanner_test.go
- internal/logs/testdata/*.jsonl
